title: Having a permissive Cross-Origin Resource Sharing policy is security-sensitive
description: Having a permissive Cross-Origin Resource Sharing policy is security-sensitiveAnalyze your codeintentionality-completesecuritySecurity Hotspotcweexpress.jsHaving a permissive Cross-Origin Resource Sharing policy is security-sensitive. It has led in the past to the following vulnerabilities:CVE-2018-0269CVE-2017-14460Same origin policyin browsers prevents, by default and for security-reasons, a javascript frontend to perform a cross-origin HTTP request to a resource that has a different origin (domain, protocol, or port) from its own. The requested target can append additional HTTP headers in response, calledCORS, that act like directives for the browser and change the access control policy / relax the same origin policy.Ask Yourself WhetherYou don’t trust the origin specified, example:Acce
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Permissive CORS policy detected - wildcard (*) allows all origins which may be a security risk
Express.js CORS header set to wildcard (*) - consider restricting to specific trusted domains
CORS header configured with wildcard (*) - this allows any origin to access the resource
CORS middleware configured to accept all origins - consider whitelisting specific domains
Critical: CORS allows credentials with wildcard origin - this is a serious security vulnerability
